title: sphaeralcea wrightii
intro: Sphaeralcea wrightii, also known as Wright's globe mallow, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a member of the mallow family and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The plant's roots and flowers have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems and skin conditions, and are believed to have anti-bacterial and anti-viral properties, providing benefits to the immune system and skin health. Sphaeralcea wrightii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in topical applications, such as creams and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
